If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** v9.3.54-0 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://test-management.browserstack.com/projects/2219752/test-runs/TR-16749/folder/13176708/86898350/1313414708 AND https://test-management.browserstack.com/projects/2219752/folder/13176708/test-cases/86898350 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** N/A **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Regression TC Execution **Device used:** Windows 11/Chrome **App Component:** Chat Report View ## Action Performed: Preconditions: Rules are enabled. Enable Attendee tracking in Rules. Make the Advertising category require attendees in category settings. 1. Go to staging.new.expensify.com 2. Naviagte to Workspace -> Click + > Create expense > Manual. 3. Enter amount > Next. 4. On the confirm page, select Advertising category and click Attendees. 5. Add one brand new attendee only (doesn't exist yet) and click Save. 6. Click Create expense. 7. Open the expense. ## Expected Result: "Multiple attendees required for this category" RBR should display ## Actual Result: No RBR "Multiple attendees required for this category" when selecting a brand new account ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7b52c4e5-0bb3-46e2-bef2-c05ae47381a7 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

Open the application in a web browser 2. Navigate to Reports > Reports 3. Use the up/down arrow keys to navigate focus ## Expected Result: When at the top or bottom of the list, pressing the up or down arrow key should immediately focus the item at the opposite end of the list. ## Actual Result: The up/down arrow keys must be pressed multiple times before the focus returns to the top or bottom of the list. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dca64735-0f66-448c-ba8a-c7820120e2ea https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/77f26b9f-3b67-47a4-b463-7894c0ec3b09 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

On tab 1 - Navigate to staging.new.expensify.com 2. Sign in to an account user A with USD currency without any policy and bank account 3. On incognito mode - From another account user B, request money to the account user A with USD currency in USD 4. Go back to user A account tab 1 5. Go offline 6.
